commit.message: chore(root): update continuity ledger for end node schema sync

feat(workflow-designer-ui): reset upstream-only types and unify text format sync

Reset enum and array subSchemas to plain string when a stale mapping is
removed, since those types can only originate from upstream mappings.
User-configurable types (number, boolean, string, object) are preserved.

Refactor the JSON→text format switch handler to reuse syncEndNodeOutput
with an empty schema instead of separate inline cleanup logic.
fix format
fix(workflow-designer-ui): scope text-format cleanup by outputId to avoid removing unrelated mappings

fix(workflow-designer-ui): address PR review feedback for end node schema sync

- Read fresh node from store to avoid overwriting concurrent content changes
- Clean up stale End node mappings when source switches to text format
- Scope schema sync by outputId to avoid corrupting mappings to other outputs
- Fix test nesting structure for single property mapping describe block

commit.message: fix format

fix(giselle): narrow __proto__ guard to only skip prototype-polluting key

Allow constructor and prototype as legitimate schema properties since
single-level assignment is safe for them. Strengthen __proto__ tests
with object payloads so they fail properly if the guard is removed.
test(giselle): use computed property syntax for __proto__ to properly exercise guard
fix(giselle): guard against dangerous keys in buildObject to prevent prototype pollution

commit.message:
fix: restore exhaustive type-check in execute-action (closes #1106)

Replace the relaxed runtime check with a proper TypeScript exhaustive
type-check using the never type. Since 'github' is currently the only
provider in the ActionData discriminated union, the default case is
correctly unreachable. This restores compile-time safety so that adding
a new provider without a corresponding case will produce a type error.
Update docs/packages-license.md
feat(deps): update agent dependency to 0.1.23

commit.message: docs(sdk): list verified schema libraries with versions in JSDoc

feat(workflow-designer-ui): remove Joi and add required() to Yup generated code

Joi lacks TypeScript type inference for schema output (always `any`),
so remove it from the validation library options. Add `.required()` to
all Yup generated fields to produce non-optional output types.
Update docs/packages-license.md

commit.message:
fix(workflow-designer-ui): escape interpolated values and improve sample code

- Add formatPropertyKey and formatStringLiteral helpers to safely escape
  property keys and enum values in generated schema code
- Add formatArkTypeLiteral for ArkType's single-quoted syntax
- Add task.status === "completed" guard in sample code
- Move schema declaration below client instantiation
Update docs/packages-license.md
feat: update agent dependencies to new runtime and builder packages
test(sdk): add typed output assertions to schema validation test
revert(workflow-designer-ui): restore original sample code generation and library options
revert(sdk): remove Symbol branding and simplify validation logic

revert: remove unnecessary optional field handling in code generators

All structured output fields are always required in Giselle, so the
optional-marking logic added by the previous commit is unreachable.

fix(sdk,workflow-designer-ui): address PR #2802 review feedback

- Skip schema validation when task is not completed (failed/cancelled)
- Add Symbol-based branding to SchemaValidationError for cross-package checks
- Fix JSDoc "Zod v4" to "Zod" for correct version reference
- Remove Yup and Joi from validation library options (unconfirmed Standard Schema v1 support)
- Guard onValueChange with runtime type check instead of force-cast
- Escape property keys and enum string literals in generated code
- Respect subSchema.required for optional fields in all code generators
- Add unit tests for schema validation (success, failure, passthrough skip, failed-task skip)

commit.message:
fix(deps): resolve webpack security vulnerabilities (dependabot #96, #97)

- Upgrade @sentry/nextjs 10.28.0 → 10.42.0 (switches to @sentry/webpack-plugin v5)
- Add webpack 5.104.1 as devDependency to override vulnerable 5.97.1 peer dep

Fixes SSRF via HTTP redirects (CVE in webpack buildHttp) and
allowedUris bypass via URL userinfo (@).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

verification.payload:
tree d1676b2cb0ae2310855f98353bbb9eb2c5d7a4bd
parent 4dd759e017bcb07e56315639fb7b601a04b91aa9
author Tadashi Shigeoka <tadashi.shigeoka@route06.co.jp> 1772759684 +0900
committer Tadashi Shigeoka <tadashi.shigeoka@route06.co.jp> 1772759684 +0900

fix(deps): resolve webpack security vulnerabilities (dependabot #96, #97)

- Upgrade @sentry/nextjs 10.28.0 → 10.42.0 (switches to @sentry/webpack-plugin v5)
- Add webpack 5.104.1 as devDependency to override vulnerable 5.97.1 peer dep

Fixes SSRF via HTTP redirects (CVE in webpack buildHttp) and
allowedUris bypass via URL userinfo (@).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message: Update docs/packages-license.md

fix(security): bump hono to 4.12.4 and @hono/node-server to 1.19.10

Update pnpm.overrides to resolve Dependabot security alerts:
- #111: SSE control field injection (CVE-2026-29085)
- #112: Static file auth bypass (CVE-2026-29045)
- #113: Cookie attribute injection (CVE-2026-29086)
- #114: @hono/node-server static path bypass (CVE-2026-29087)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

verification.payload:

releases.name: v0.71.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed

### New Features
- feat(sdk): add Standard Schema v1 validation support to runAndWait (#2802) @WashizuRyo

### Bug Fixes
- fix(giselle): replace Object.create(null) with plain objects for RSC serialization (#2815) @WashizuRyo
- fix(structured-output): remove array type from End Node structured output picker (#2816) @WashizuRyo
- fix: restore exhaustive type-check in execute-action (#2814) @sputnik-mac

### Dependencies
- feat(deps): update agent dependency to 0.1.23 (#2813) @toyamarinyon
- update @giselles-ai/agent to v0.1.24 (#2817) @toyamarinyon

Originally released: 2026-03-10

readme_html:
the AI agent studio powering product delivery

▶︎▶︎ The YouTube video with audio can be found here ◀︎◀︎

👋 Introduction

Giselle is an open source AI for agentic workflows, enabling seamless human-AI collaboration.

⚡ Quick Start

Get Giselle running locally in under 2 minutes:

# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/giselles-ai/giselle.git
cd giselle

# Install dependencies
pnpm install

# Create environment file
touch .env.local

# Add your API key (at least one required)
echo 'OPENAI_API_KEY="your_openai_api_key_here"' >> .env.local

# Start development server
pnpm turbo dev

Open http://localhost:3000 and start building your AI agents!

Note: You need at least one AI provider API key. Supported providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI.

✨ Features

                   

  • ⚡ GitHub AI Operations - Automates issues, PRs, and deployments with AI
  • 🎨 Visual Agent Builder - Create and modify agents in minutes using an intuitive drag-and-drop interface
  • 🤖 Multi-Model Composition - Leverage GPT, Claude, Gemini, and more—agents select the best model for each task
  • 📁 Knowledge Store - Access and search your code and data from one place. GitHub vector store integration supported
  • 👥 Team Collaboration - Design agents collaboratively with shared configurations and contextual awareness (In Development)
  • 🚀 Template Hub - Kickstart projects with one-click agent templates—contributed by the community (In Development)

🎯 Use Cases

  • 📚 Research Assistant - Automatically gather information from web and internal docs
  • 🔍 Code Reviewer - AI-powered code review that integrates with your GitHub workflow
  • 📄 Document Generator - Auto-create PRDs, specs, and release notes from your codebase
  • 🔄 Workflow Automator - Chain multiple AI models to handle complex business processes

🚀 Using Giselle

☁️ Cloud

We host Giselle as a cloud service for anyone to use instantly. It has all the same features as the self-hosted version, and includes 30 minutes of free Agent time per month in the free plan.

🏠 Self-hosting

Follow this starter guide to get Giselle running in your environment.

🎵 Vibe Coding Guide

If you're using AI coding assistants like Claude, Cursor, or WindSurf to help build with Giselle, check out our Vibe Coding Guide. This guide explains:

  • What is vibe coding and how to approach it effectively
  • How to set up your Node.js environment and install dependencies
  • Understanding Giselle's project structure
  • Running the playground and connecting to LLM providers

Designed for both developers and non-engineers, this guide will help you harness the power of AI to build with Giselle without needing traditional coding expertise.

🗺️ Roadmap

Giselle is currently still in active development. The roadmap for the public repository is currently being created, and once it's finalized, we will update this README accordingly.

🤝 Contributing

Your contributions — big or small — help Giselle evolve and improve. Interested in joining us?

Here's how you can contribute:

  • Star this repo ⭐
  • Follow us on social media: Facebook, X, Instagram and YouTube
  • Report a bug you encounter while using Giselle
  • Request a feature you think would be helpful
  • Submit a pull request if you'd like to add new features or fix bugs

For more details, please see our contributing guide.

📄 License

Giselle is licensed under the Apache License Version 2.0.

Licenses for third-party packages can be found in docs/packages-license.md.
